2023-08-01.title: Putin’s Crackdown Leaves Transgender Russians Bracing for Worse
2023-08-01.abstract: A new law underscores how Vladimir V. Putin is increasingly using the war in Ukraine as justification for greater restrictions on L.G.B.T.Q. life, portraying it as a consequence of deviant Western values.

2023-08-01.title: Tropical Cyclone Kills 2 in Southern Japan
2023-08-01.abstract: Typhoon Khanun was hovering near Okinawa Prefecture days after an earlier tropical cyclone, Doksuri, brought death and destruction to China and the Philippines.

2023-08-01.title: Aung San Suu Kyi’s Prison Sentence Reduced in Myanmar
2023-08-01.abstract: The civilian leader, who was ousted in a coup in 2021, had six years cut from a 33-year prison sentence and five of her 19 convictions overturned, state television said.

2023-08-01.title: ‘Barbenheimer’ Isn’t Funny in Nuclear-Scarred Japan
2023-08-01.abstract: The Japanese subsidiary of Warner Bros. criticized what it called insensitive promotion of “Barbie” by the company’s headquarters.

2023-08-01.title: Mexico’s ‘Monster’ Trucks Show Cartels Taking Drug War to Next Level
2023-08-01.abstract: Taking the monster truck concept to deadly new realms, cartels are modifying pickups with gun turrets, steel armoring and battering rams.

2023-08-02.title: An Aging Pope Francis Looks to ‘Youth Day’ to Energize Church’s Future
2023-08-02.abstract: The pope will preside over a gathering of young people from around the world, mindful that the changes he has initiated in the Roman Catholic Church will depend on them.

2023-08-02.title: Under Fire and Understaffed: The Fight to Save Ukraine’s Wounded
2023-08-02.abstract: Positioned close to the front lines, temporary medical posts are a critical lifeline for the constant inflow of soldiers hurt in the counteroffensive.

2023-08-02.title: City in Mexico Bans Performances of Songs With Misogynistic Lyrics
2023-08-02.abstract: The city of Chihuahua said it would impose hefty fines on bands that perform songs with lyrics that “promote violence against women.”

2023-08-02.title: The Face of Israel’s Protests Is a Particle Physicist
2023-08-02.abstract: Shikma Bressler, a mother of five who says her passion is life in the lab, has emerged as a symbol of the struggle against the government’s divisive judicial overhaul.

2023-08-02.title: Xi’s Surprise Shake-Up Exposes Problems at Top of China’s Nuclear Force
2023-08-02.abstract: The Chinese leader’s replacement of two commanders has fanned speculation about corruption or failings in the force that manages China’s nuclear missiles.

2023-08-03.title: Scion of Family Tied to 2014 Ferry Disaster Is Extradited to South Korea
2023-08-03.abstract: Yoo Hyuk-kee’s family company ran the Sewol ferry, whose sinking off the South Korean coast left hundreds dead. Prosecutors have charged Mr. Yoo, a U.S. resident, with embezzlement.

2023-08-03.title: ‘Not Another Coup as Usual’: What to Know About Niger’s Crisis
2023-08-03.abstract: Amid an unpredictable military takeover, neighboring countries have threatened to go to war — some to scuttle the president’s ouster, and others to ensure its success.

2023-08-03.title: Pastor or Traitor? Ukrainians Shun a Church Seen as a Kremlin Tool.
2023-08-03.abstract: The village parishioners’ decision to oust their priest reflects a broader push within Ukraine to reduce the influence of an Orthodox church that answers to Moscow.

2023-08-03.title: Heat Illness Sickens Hundreds at Scout Jamboree in South Korea
2023-08-03.abstract: Tens of thousands of Scouts came to the gathering. More than 100 were hospitalized with heat-related symptoms.

2023-08-03.title: Australia Will Return Looted Sculptures to Cambodia
2023-08-03.abstract: A centuries-old Buddhist sculpture was taken from a rural area in Cambodia nearly 30 years ago. It was later sold with two other statues to the National Gallery of Australia.

2023-08-04.title: Australians’ ‘Golden Ticket’ to the U.S.
2023-08-04.abstract: Proposed changes to the E-3 visa have worried some expatriates.

2023-08-04.abstract: Aleksei A. Navalny, who is already in prison, was sentenced for supporting “extremism,” amid intensified suppression of dissent in Russia.

2023-08-04.abstract: After the Kakhovka dam was blown up in June, the reservoir above it quickly drained, revealing a bonanza of artifacts that has electrified Ukrainian archaeologists.

2023-08-04.abstract: The military junta that ousted the president in the West African nation of Niger last week also dismissed Niger’s ambassadors to key partners: the United States, France and Nigeria.

2023-08-05.abstract: Some 40 countries that have largely remained on the sidelines of the war were invited to talks on Saturday in Saudi Arabia, as Ukraine redoubles international efforts to isolate Moscow.

2023-08-05.abstract: The Times unraveled a financial network that stretches from Chicago to Shanghai and uses American nonprofits to push Chinese talking points worldwide.

2023-08-05.abstract: In addition to the strike on an oil tanker, Ukraine issued a warning that commercial ships using any of six Russian Black Sea ports would be considered military targets.

2023-08-05.abstract: The British, Singaporean and U.S. scouting organizations cited health concerns for their departure from a much-anticipated youth event in South Korea.

2023-08-05.abstract: A recent military takeover in Niger has thrown into uncertainty the future of France’s presence in West and Central Africa, raising doubts about the future of U.S. military involvement there, too.

2023-08-06.title: Woman Dies After Recent Mass Attack in South Korea
2023-08-06.abstract: The victim was one of 14 people hurt in a car-and-stabbing rampage on Thursday in a city near Seoul. She had been hit by the car.

2023-08-06.abstract: A group of Swiss women over 64 has filed a lawsuit against Switzerland with Europe’s top human rights court, saying the country has violated their rights by failing to curb emissions.

2023-08-06.abstract: Ukraine has grown bolder, firing drones at Moscow, while Russian forces have turned to regular attacks on the Black Sea, a crucial economic lifeline.

2023-08-06.abstract: The movie has exceeded box office expectations, as China’s female moviegoers celebrate a film that addresses women’s rights head-on.

2023-08-06.title: Putin’s Forever War

2023-08-07.title: Residents Rush to Help After Deadly Train Derailment in Pakistan
2023-08-07.abstract: Rescue teams and army personnel combed through the wreckage in Nawabsha, Pakistan, with the help of people from nearby villages.

2023-08-07.abstract: Xi Jinping, China’s leader, set out to clean up the military a decade ago. But now his crown jewel, the missile force, is under a shadow.

2023-08-07.abstract: Singles are signing up in droves for city-hosted blind-dating events, but some point to their inability to address real issues with family planning.

2023-08-08.abstract: The clash, in which a Chinese ship blocked 2 Philippine vessels, is the latest in a hotly contested region of the South China Sea.

2023-08-08.abstract: The storm, which battered Okinawa last week, engulfed the southern Japanese island of Kyushu on its way toward the Korean Peninsula.

2023-08-08.title: Ireland Says Goodbye to Sinéad O’Connor

2023-08-08.abstract: The United Arab Emirates, which has translated its wealth into outsize global influence, is diverging from U.S. foreign policy — particularly when it comes to isolating Russia and limiting ties with China.

2023-08-08.abstract: The Black Sea, a largely overlooked part of the war in Ukraine, is suddenly a cauldron of military and geopolitical tensions. The region is deeply important to Moscow, Ukraine and the West.

2023-08-09.abstract: The three guests, and a fourth who fell ill, had symptoms consistent with mushroom poisoning, the police say. The woman who served the meal has been questioned by the police.

2023-08-09.abstract: Britain’s vegetable producers are hoping this is a moment for the humble frozen pea, a cost-effective staple at a time of rising food prices.

2023-08-09.abstract: A drone assault on a Danube River port sent shock waves — both the physical and the psychological kind — into villages just across the water that are in NATO territory.

2023-08-09.abstract: Russian officials said one person was killed and dozens were injured in the blast. The warehouse is on the grounds of a plant that has produced night vision devices and binoculars, according to local news media.

2023-08-09.abstract: The investigation the two agents were part of grew into a larger inquiry that netted members of an online pedophile ring in the U.S. and Australia.

2023-08-10.abstract: After pummeling southern Japan again, the storm moved slowly up the Korean Peninsula, bringing torrential rain and winds. One person died in floodwaters.

2023-08-10.abstract: The candidate, Fernando Villavicencio, had been vocal about ties between the state and organized crime, in a country roiled by violence tied to drug trafficking.

2023-08-10.abstract: The presidential candidate who was assassinated on Wednesday had a long history in Ecuador’s public affairs, largely as an antagonist to those in power.

2023-08-11.abstract: Flavorless (?) Candy, a convenience store product in Japan, spurred debate over what the “taste of nothingness” tastes like, if anything.

2023-08-11.abstract: Day by day, the People’s Liberation Army is turning up the pressure, deploying an ever-wider array of planes and ships.

2023-08-11.abstract: Ksenia Sobchak, one of the best known media figures still in Russia, says her fatalistic stance just reflects a grim reality. Some liberals call her a Kremlin stooge, while hawks see her as disloyal.

2023-08-11.abstract: The torture rooms in the battered Ukrainian city of Kupiansk have been empty for months. But Moscow’s advancing forces are just five miles away.

2023-08-12.abstract: Turkey’s largest city is dotted with the tombs of religious figures, where pilgrims seek divine intervention. “When you ask for something from God, those who are beloved by God can be a go-between.”

2023-08-12.abstract: An outspoken critic of Beijing, Lai Ching-te aims to reassure voters and Washington that he will be a safe pair of hands if elected.

2023-08-12.abstract: Ontario’s auditor general found that the process to open up land in Toronto’s Greenbelt was influenced by and favored two developers.

2023-08-13.abstract: While an invasive insect bears much of the blame for endangering a beloved symbol of Rome, critics are also pointing the finger at city government.

2023-08-13.abstract: Ukrainian officials said two children and their parents were among the dead after the attacks on Sunday.

2023-08-13.abstract: The rock ballad was a cult classic in China. But what if the party wanted to use it to spread “positive energy”?

2023-08-13.abstract: Most military experts doubt that they would have, and say that Kyiv can still prevail without them.

2023-08-14.abstract: Denounced in some Middle Eastern countries for undermining traditional gender norms, the hit movie is finding an audience in Saudi Arabia, illustrating the region’s shifting political landscape.

2023-08-14.abstract: Two years on from when the Taliban took Kabul, the outflow of young Talibs has renewed fears of violent extremism spilling across Afghanistan’s borders.

2023-08-15.abstract: Reports from news agencies said at least 35 people were killed and 66 hospitalized in Dagestan, but there were no immediate reports of foul play or of a connection to the war in Ukraine.

2023-08-15.title: Ukraine’s Unseen Army: The Mechanics Who Keep the Tanks Rolling
2023-08-15.abstract: Behind the thousands of troops fighting the counteroffensive are engineers and technicians who perform an essential, and often dangerous, job.

2023-08-15.abstract: Facing a growing mental health crisis among soldiers, Ukraine’s therapists and doctors use a variety of treatments, but the need far outstrips the available care.

2023-08-15.abstract: The heavy rains and winds brought by the storm prompted evacuation warnings and the cancellation of hundreds of flights.

2023-08-16.abstract: The former British prime minister, who left Downing Street widely unpopular, is back in favor with his party, Labour, which hopes his political skills can be an advantage as an election nears.

2023-08-16.abstract: As temperatures rise, a vast but aging system is feeling the strains of rising energy costs, increasing water scarcity and mounting pressure on public budgets.

2023-08-16.abstract: A 40-mile channel, best known outside shipping circles as a magnet for bird watchers, is now a crucial route allowing Ukrainian grain to reach the sea, protected by a NATO umbrella.

2023-08-16.abstract: Russian drones hit the ports of Reni and Izmail and damaged warehouses used to export grain, Ukrainian officials said.

2023-08-16.abstract: Pita Limjaroenrat won a surprise victory in the general election, but his attempts to form a government and become the nation’s next premier have been thwarted.

2023-08-17.abstract: The animals are dying in droves in the Black Sea, and Ukrainian officials are documenting the deaths, hoping to prosecute Russia for the war’s ecological toll.

2023-08-17.abstract: A presidential candidate’s assassination has focused global attention on the country’s extraordinary bloodshed fueled by powerful international and domestic crime groups.

2023-08-17.abstract: Officials told residents to clear out of the Northwest Territories city of Yellowknife by Friday afternoon. Most were expected to flee south by road.

2023-08-17.abstract: Brian Houston, who once led Hillsong, was charged because he did not inform the police after his father admitted in 1999 to sexually assaulting a young boy decades before.

2023-08-17.abstract: The vessel carrying mostly Senegalese nationals was found off the coast of the West African archipelago. Thirty-eight people survived.

2023-08-18.title: Allies to Be Allowed to Send F-16s to Ukraine, U.S. Official Says
2023-08-18.abstract: The jets can be sent once Ukrainian pilots are trained to operate them.

2023-08-18.abstract: The housing market in China is in turmoil. But more and more women, facing a less equal society, are buying their own homes in search of security.

2023-08-18.title: Up the Matildas!
2023-08-18.abstract: Australia’s national women’s soccer team have walked away with the nation’s hearts clasped firmly in their hands.

2023-08-18.abstract: Using an ultralight aircraft, Johannes Fritz once taught endangered ibises a migration path over the Alps. Because of climate change, he is now showing them a much longer route to a winter’s refuge.

2023-08-19.abstract: The joint air and sea drills, widely expected in response to a presidential candidate’s visit to the United States, so far appear to be relatively limited.

2023-08-19.abstract: The women play Spain on Sunday, hoping to end a nearly six-decade national wait for a World Cup win — a reminder of the travails of the Three Lions, the country’s long-suffering men’s team.

2023-08-19.abstract: More than a million people in France have attended a “Climate Fresk” class to understand the process of global warming. The project is now spreading abroad.

2023-08-19.abstract: Even as the fighting in Syria has largely stalled, more than a decade of conflict has left behind an array of unexploded munitions like cluster bombs. An earthquake in February heightened the risks.

2023-08-20.abstract: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine said the Netherlands had agreed to supply 42 of the jets. Denmark also said it would donate the planes.

2023-08-20.abstract: A former first lady and an anticorruption candidate are on the ballot in a runoff contest.

2023-08-20.abstract: Despite tough fighting and heavy casualties, Ukrainian commanders say their forces are in better shape now than just months ago, while Russian troops appear worse off.

2023-08-20.abstract: The final against England brought out fans of all stripes and rallied girls in both countries to hit the field and play.

2023-08-21.abstract: The landslide victory by the candidate of an upstart party signals an uncertain new chapter in Central America’s largest country.

2023-08-21.abstract: Luisa González and Daniel Noboa were the top candidates to emerge from Sunday’s election in a country where declining security has been the leading issue for most voters.

2023-08-21.abstract: A Human Rights Watch report says the guards regularly fire on African migrants trying to enter the kingdom from Yemen and killed hundreds in a 15-month period.

2023-08-21.title: The BRICS Club of Emerging Nations Debates Letting Others In
2023-08-21.abstract: China wants to expand the five-nation bloc, but the members’ conflicting interests may get in the way.

2023-08-21.abstract: Ms. Kennedy’s recent swim in the Pacific waters where J.F.K. survived a wartime ordeal shows how her family legacy informs her work.

2023-08-22.abstract: Gen. Hun Manet is expected to guide a generational shift in Cambodian politics, taking over for his father Hun Sen, one of the world’s longest-ruling prime ministers.

2023-08-22.abstract: Months of political chaos have culminated in the selection of Srettha Thavisin, a businessman seen as amenable to royalists and the military-appointed Senate.

2023-08-22.abstract: Polish authorities have issued dire warnings that the Suwalki Gap, on Poland’s northern border between Russia and Belarus, is under threat. Locals say that is just election-related fear-mongering.

2023-08-22.abstract: Nurses, doctors and workers of all kinds are seeking to escape the country’s economic turmoil, an issue that has become a central theme in the election scheduled for Wednesday.

2023-08-23.abstract: Drone attacks in Russian territory have become more frequent as Ukraine wages a grueling counteroffensive.

2023-08-23.abstract: The Times tracked several months of Russian state messaging in the Kremlin’s effort to recruit soldiers.

2023-08-23.abstract: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, pitches his country as a leader of the developing world, rallying support for an alternative to the U.S.-dominated global order.

2023-08-23.abstract: A rescue effort was underway as reports indicated that several additional workers were trapped under the wreckage.

2023-08-24.abstract: All 10 people on a jet linked to Yevgeny V. Prigozhin, the founder of the mercenary group, were killed on Wednesday, Russian officials said.

2023-08-24.title: Japan Begins Releasing Treated Radioactive Water at Fukushima
2023-08-24.abstract: China said it would suspend imports of Japanese seafood in response to what it has called an unsafe plan to dispose of the wastewater.

2023-08-24.title: Ukraine Observes Independence Day With Subdued Celebrations
2023-08-24.abstract: The Ukrainian authorities said the national holiday, which comes exactly 18 months after Russia’s full-scale invasion, won’t include public celebrations amid concerns about Russian attacks.

2023-08-24.title: Escape China by Jet Ski? A Dissident Is Said to Have Planned It for Years
2023-08-24.abstract: Officials in South Korea aren’t saying who crossed 200 miles of ocean to reach its shores. But people who know Kwon Pyong say it wasn’t his first audacious move.

2023-08-24.title: After Thais Vote to Move Forward, the Old Guard Just Shifts Its Grip
2023-08-24.abstract: The country went months without naming a new prime minister, only for Parliament to elect Srettha Thavisin, a candidate who many frustrated voters say represents the establishment.

2023-08-25.title: The 1,300-Year Search for the Loch Ness Monster
2023-08-25.abstract: An expedition to find the creature will take place this weekend in Scotland. Here’s a look at previous attempts.

2023-08-25.title: Yevgeny Prigozhin, Renegade Mercenary Chief Who Rattled Kremlin
2023-08-25.abstract: A tycoon and a Putin ally, he built a paramilitary force that fought by Russia’s side even as he castigated its military leaders. He died at 62 in a plane crash, Russian authorities said.

2023-08-25.title: He Tracks Elusive Amazon Tribes, but Only From the Shadows
2023-08-25.abstract: Jair Candor combs the forest for Indigenous people who want no contact with outsiders. The goal: to prove they exist so their land can be legally protected.

2023-08-25.title: Seafood Is Safe After Fukushima Water Dump, but Some Won’t Eat It
2023-08-25.abstract: Sushi is among several shunned foods as Japan dumps treated radioactive water into the Pacific. Experts say the fear is irrational but understandable.

2023-08-25.title: Two Arrested After Suspected Arson at Britain’s ‘Wonkiest’ Pub
2023-08-25.abstract: The Crooked House pub in England’s West Midlands went up in flames earlier this month. Two men have now been taken into custody on suspicion of arson.

2023-08-26.title: Ukraine Is Still Grappling With the Battlefield Prigozhin Left Behind
2023-08-26.abstract: He shored up Russian forces at their most vulnerable and drew Ukraine into a costly fight for Bakhmut, giving Moscow time to build defenses that are slowing Ukraine’s counteroffensive.

2023-08-26.title: He Fled China’s Repression. But China’s Long Arm Got Him in Another Country.
2023-08-26.abstract: Beijing has become more aggressive in its pursuit of dissidents overseas, as highlighted by the arrest of a Chinese rights lawyer in Laos.

2023-08-26.title: In Push to Modernize Cairo, Cultural Gems and Green Spaces Razed
2023-08-26.abstract: The Egyptian government has demolished historic tombs, cultural centers, artisan workshops and gardens in pursuit of large-scale urban renewal.

2023-08-26.title: ‘He Has Blood on His Hands’
2023-08-26.abstract: In a Ukraine village, there are no tears for Yevgeny V. Prigozhin, the leader of the Wagner private militia, whose presumed death in a plane crash was reported this week.

2023-08-26.title: FIFA Suspends Spanish Soccer Federation Chief Involved in Post-Game Kiss
2023-08-26.abstract: The kiss and its aftermath have stirred debate over the treatment of women in Spanish soccer.

2023-08-27.title: Thailand’s Weed Industry Is Poised to Grow Fast
2023-08-27.abstract: The country’s legal marijuana business — a rarity in Asia — is struggling with oversupply, illegal imports and regulatory ambiguity. Investors are piling in anyway.

2023-08-27.title: Three U.S. Marines Die in Air Crash in Australia
2023-08-27.abstract: An Osprey aircraft with 23 people on board crashed during a joint training exercise near Darwin, in the north of Australia. Several others were seriously injured.

2023-08-27.title: Russia Says Ukraine Fired Drones at Three Regions
2023-08-27.abstract: The frequency of drone attacks inside Russia has increased in recent weeks, bringing the war to areas deep inside Russian territory.

2023-08-27.title: Western Banks Help Fund Blacklisted Oligarch’s Charity
2023-08-27.abstract: Konstantin Malofeyev’s foundation funds orphanages in occupied Ukraine. He denies that a child-removal program there is a war crime: “All of this is fake.”

2023-08-27.title: A Former French President Gives a Voice to Obstinate Russian Sympathies
2023-08-27.abstract: Remarks by Nicolas Sarkozy have raised fears that Europe’s pro-Putin chorus may grow louder as Ukraine’s plodding counteroffensive puts pressure on Western resolve.

2023-08-28.title: Pastor Leads Followers Into Gunfire While Protesting Haiti Gangs
2023-08-28.abstract: Pastor Marco, an evangelical church leader, told his followers that they were bulletproof as they marched against a gang on the outskirts of Port-au-Prince, a witness said.

2023-08-28.title: They’re Forced to Run Online Scams. Their Captors Are Untouchable.
2023-08-28.abstract: Gangs in Cambodia have compelled thousands of captives to defraud unsuspecting victims. Because of political connections, no one is stopping them.

2023-08-28.title: To Escape the Heat in Dubai, Head to the Beach at Midnight
2023-08-28.abstract: In a city where weather that would constitute a deadly heat wave in Europe is just a typical summer day, official “night beaches” have become a popular way to cool down.

2023-08-28.title: Libya’s Foreign Minister Flees Country Amid Uproar Over Meeting With Israeli
2023-08-28.abstract: The minister, Najla el-Mangoush, flew to Turkey after reports emerged that she had met with her Israeli counterpart, sparking unrest in several Libyan cities.

2023-08-28.title: Russia Charges Former U.S. Consulate Worker With Collecting Information About Ukraine War
2023-08-28.abstract: Robert Shonov, a Russian national, faces up to eight years in prison. The U.S. said the allegations against him are “wholly without merit.”

2023-08-29.title: China’s Economic Outlook: Pep Talks Up Top, Gloom on the Ground
2023-08-29.abstract: Beijing has characterized concerns about the economic slowdown as being inflated by Western critics. Widespread anxiety and pessimism paint a different picture.

2023-08-29.title: Imran Khan’s 3-Year Sentence Is Suspended by Pakistan Appeals Court
2023-08-29.abstract: Mr. Khan, who was ousted as prime minister last year, still faces dozens of court cases that could keep him behind bars.

2023-08-29.title: U.K. Air Travel Will Be Disrupted for ‘Some Days’ After Traffic Control Glitch
2023-08-29.abstract: The government ruled out a cyberattack as the cause of the technical problems, which forced airlines to cancel about 5 percent of the flights in and out of the country on Tuesday as they tried to catch up.

2023-08-29.title: Wagner Says Its Chief Has Been Buried, Ending Days of Speculation
2023-08-29.abstract: Details about the funeral for Yevgeny Prigozhin, who died in a plane crash last week along with nine other people, were murky.

2023-08-29.title: Preyed On by Gangs: Gun Violence Surges Within Israel’s Arab Communities
2023-08-29.abstract: At least 155 Arab citizens have died this year in violence largely attributed to organized crime. Some say the government has not done enough to stop the killing.

2023-08-30.title: Will a Bitterly Divided Australia Elevate the Voice of Aboriginal People?
2023-08-30.abstract: A referendum to set up an Indigenous advisory body in Parliament was envisioned as uniting the country. The opposite has happened.

2023-08-30.title: Russian Airport Hit by Drone Attack
2023-08-30.abstract: The governor of Pskov, Russia, shared videos on his Telegram account showing smoke rising from the city’s airport and rounds being fired into the sky.

2023-08-30.title: Tested in Greece’s Fires: An Emergency Force for 27 Countries
2023-08-30.abstract: The European Union deployed firefighters and equipment in what could be a preview of how it handles disasters linked to climate change.

2023-08-30.title: In a Storied River, Fish Are Dying in Droves as Climate Change Scorches Canada
2023-08-30.abstract: The effects of a summer of environmental catastrophes were felt everywhere in Canada, from its vast boreal forests to a river on Vancouver Island once fished by Hollywood royalty.

2023-08-30.title: Macron Tries Outreach to Head Off Further Protests
2023-08-30.abstract: After a tumultuous year, the French president is meeting with his opponents in hopes of building bridges. But few seem interested in working with him.

2023-08-31.title: An Obsolete German Tank Seeks a Second Life on Ukraine’s Front Lines
2023-08-31.abstract: The Cold War-era Leopard 1A5 may be old, but it is still effective, Germany says. The country is now training Ukrainian troops to operate the weapons.

2023-08-31.title: Rare Protests in Syria Summon Echoes of Arab Spring
2023-08-31.abstract: After 12 years of conflict, anger over growing economic hardships has boiled over. Protesters are demanding the ouster of the authoritarian President Bashar al-Assad and an end to the war.

2023-08-31.title: A Resurgent Labour Party Sees Scotland as a Springboard to Power
2023-08-31.abstract: As the Scottish nationalists stumble, a by-election near Glasgow this fall will test support for the opposition Labour party ahead of Britain’s coming general election.

2023-08-31.title: China’s Disinformation Fuels Anger Over Fukushima Water Release
2023-08-31.abstract: By exaggerating the risks from Japan’s discharge of treated wastewater, Beijing hopes to cast Japan and its allies as conspirators in malfeasance, analysts say.

2023-08-31.title: Building Fire in Johannesburg Kills Dozens
2023-08-31.abstract: The blaze consumed an abandoned five-story building that had become a sprawling informal settlement, officials said.
